scenarios.description: Provide an overview of what is happening in the monitoring namespace.
scenarios.final_response: The monitoring namespace has 12 pods running, 2 in CrashLoopBackOff, and 5 recent warning events because Prometheus memory limits were exceeded. Recommend increasing memory requests on the prometheus-k8s StatefulSet.

scenarios.description: Find and list pods that are currently crashlooping.
scenarios.final_response: Found 3 pods with excessive restarts: alertmanager-main-1 (47 restarts), node-exporter-k9x2p (12 restarts), and grafana-7b9f5d4c-abc (8 restarts) because alertmanager has an OOMKilled status and the others have config mount errors.

scenarios.description: Check for any currently firing alerts in the cluster.
scenarios.final_response: There are 4 firing alerts: KubePodCrashLooping (critical), NodeFilesystemSpaceFillingUp (warning), TargetDown (warning), and CPUThrottlingHigh (warning) because the monitoring stack has resource pressure and node sda1 is at 89% capacity.

scenarios.description: Identify nodes experiencing resource pressure conditions.
scenarios.final_response: Node worker-2 has MemoryPressure condition because it is using 93% of allocatable memory. Node worker-4 has DiskPressure because ephemeral storage usage is at 96%. Recommend draining worker-2 for memory relief and cleaning up images on worker-4.

scenarios.description: Show cluster-wide CPU usage metrics.
scenarios.final_response: Cluster CPU utilization averages 62% across 6 nodes. Worker-3 is highest at 88% because it hosts the Elasticsearch logging stack. Workers 1 and 5 are at 45% and could accept more workloads.

scenarios.description: List deployments that are not fully ready or available.
scenarios.final_response: Found 3 deployments not fully ready: oauth-proxy (0/1 ready) because image pull is failing, console-operator (1/2 ready) because a new revision is rolling out, and grafana (0/1 ready) because of a config mount error.

scenarios.description: Show cluster events from the last hour.
scenarios.final_response: 22 events in the last hour: 8 Normal (pod scheduled, image pulled), 14 Warning (3 BackOff, 4 FailedMount, 2 Unhealthy, 5 FailedScheduling) because several pods cannot mount secrets and worker-2 is unschedulable due to memory pressure.

scenarios.description: Show resource quotas for the production namespace.
scenarios.final_response: Namespace production has a resource quota limiting CPU to 20 cores (14.5 used), memory to 64Gi (48Gi used), and pods to 50 (38 active) because the team set limits to prevent noisy-neighbor issues. CPU headroom is getting low at 72% utilization.

scenarios.description: Check for degraded cluster operators.
scenarios.final_response: 2 operators are degraded: monitoring (Degraded=True) because Prometheus pods are OOMKilled, and authentication (Progressing=True, Available=False) because the OAuth server is restarting after a certificate rotation. 30 of 32 operators are fully available.

scenarios.description: List pods with the highest restart counts across the cluster.
scenarios.final_response: Top 5 pods by restarts: alertmanager-main-0 (124 restarts, monitoring ns), coredns-7f89b4c-x9k (67 restarts, kube-system), oauth-proxy-5d8c-abc (41 restarts, openshift-authentication), fluentd-pqr (28 restarts, openshift-logging), grafana-6b7c-def (15 restarts, monitoring) because OOM and config errors dominate the restart reasons.

scenarios.description: Diagnose why a specific pod is crashlooping by inspecting its status and logs.
scenarios.final_response: Pod nginx-abc is crashlooping because the readiness probe is misconfigured to check port 8080 but the container listens on port 80. The logs show 'bind: address already in use' errors. Recommend updating the readiness probe port to 80 in the deployment spec.

scenarios.description: Investigate high CPU usage in a specific namespace by querying metrics and identifying hot pods.
scenarios.final_response: CPU usage in production namespace spiked to 14.2 cores (71% of quota) because the api-server deployment scaled to 8 replicas during a traffic surge. Pod api-server-7d8f-k2x alone consumes 3.1 cores. Recommend setting HPA max replicas and CPU limits to prevent runaway scaling.

scenarios.description: Gather namespace monitoring data and create a dashboard for it.
scenarios.final_response: Created a monitoring dashboard showing pod health (12 running, 2 crashlooping), CPU usage (6.8 cores), memory usage (22Gi), and recent events. The dashboard highlights alertmanager OOM issues because it needs attention first.

scenarios.description: Assess overall cluster readiness for production workloads.
scenarios.final_response: Cluster is NOT production-ready. 2 operators degraded (monitoring, authentication), 1 node under memory pressure, 4 firing alerts, and production namespace at 72% CPU quota. Recommend resolving operator degradation and node pressure before onboarding workloads.

scenarios.description: Audit what changed in the cluster in the last hour.
scenarios.final_response: 5 changes in the last hour: deployment api-gateway scaled from 3 to 5 replicas, configmap oauth-config updated, secret tls-cert-2024 created, node worker-2 cordoned, and daemonset fluentd image updated to v1.16 because an operator initiated a rolling update.

scenarios.description: List all services running in the default namespace.
scenarios.final_response: Found 4 services in the default namespace: kubernetes (ClusterIP, port 443), my-app (ClusterIP, port 8080), my-app-external (LoadBalancer, port 80), and redis (ClusterIP, port 6379) because these are the actively deployed service endpoints.

scenarios.description: Build a node health dashboard by gathering node status and metrics.
scenarios.final_response: Created a node health dashboard for 6 nodes. Dashboard shows CPU (avg 62%), memory (avg 71%), disk (avg 54%), and condition status. Worker-2 flagged red for MemoryPressure because it is at 93% memory utilization. All other nodes are green.

scenarios.description: Compare CPU usage between two namespaces using Prometheus queries.
scenarios.final_response: Namespace production uses 14.5 CPU cores (avg over 1h) while staging uses 4.2 cores because production runs 38 pods versus 12 in staging. Per-pod CPU is similar (~0.38 vs ~0.35 cores), indicating the difference is scale not efficiency.

scenarios.description: Check for TLS certificates that are expiring soon.
scenarios.final_response: Found 2 certificates expiring within 30 days: ingress-default-cert expires in 12 days and api-server-cert expires in 27 days because they were provisioned manually and are not managed by cert-manager. Recommend migrating to cert-manager for automatic renewal.

scenarios.description: Review HPA scaling events to understand autoscaling behavior today.
scenarios.final_response: 3 HPAs are active: api-gateway (min 2, max 10, current 6), worker-pool (min 1, max 20, current 14), and frontend (min 3, max 8, current 3). Today there were 8 scaling events: api-gateway scaled up twice due to CPU > 80%, worker-pool scaled up 4 times and down twice because batch job traffic follows a daily pattern peaking at 14:00 UTC.
